evidence: Both task-run constructors in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:288-297` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:338-347` build lineage entries without any explicit `milestone_id`, so the record still relies on the storage path for milestone context.
findings_or_gaps: Requirement miss: `TaskRunEntry` still does not capture an explicit `milestone_id`. The task prompt called this out as required for self-contained lineage queries, but the model and write paths never serialize it (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:268-288`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:288-297`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:338-347`).
follow_up_or_amendments: Add an optional `milestone_id` field to `TaskRunEntry` with serde defaults for backward compatibility, populate it from both `record_bead_start` and `record_bead_completion`, and preserve it through `update_task_run` rewrites.
Extend the serialization/backward-compat coverage to include `milestone_id` presence and absence, then rerun `cargo test --lib contexts::milestone_record` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`.
